target_section: NEW part8b in sections.json — AI & Conclusions tab, between part8 and part9
summary: Part 8b drafted as point-by-point response to the dome author's 'What A Fair Auditor Should Actually Test' 5-item list (verified verbatim against live review-response.html on 2026-04-18). Adoption verdicts: Point 1 (claim_keys) ADOPTED PARTIAL — claim_key annotations yes, 'collisions resolved' no, because visible registry still shows duplicate WIN-NNN labels. Point 2 (strongest geometry path) ADOPTED FULL as steel-man audit — four-row sequenced-read table at WIN-069, with explicit note that this is a narrative audit, not an independence check (Kill-Shot #1 unchanged). Point 3 (layered eclipse) ADOPTED FULL — four-row L1-L4 stack, explicit Tier 3 pass/fail threshold (-17 to -21 nT, >80% coverage, >=3 independent sites, pre-registered instruments), ensemble anchor labeled as calibration layer not anchor. Point 4 (WIN-058 pending + resolution criteria) ADOPTED FULL — our WIN-058 and WIN-058b already reflect Misleading/Not Demonstrated, we add explicit resolution criteria for WIN-058b; dated history preserved. Point 5 ('only current wording') REJECTED with reasoned replacement standard (dated citations + always-current engagement + preserved change log) — the rejection is surgical, accepts 'engage current wording' and rejects 'only current wording'; replacement is a symmetric rule stricter than what was asked. Structural-meta section 8b.6 notes the fair-auditor list landed in the same 2026-04-17T16:21:52Z multi-file window as three API-level concessions (methodology.json OTS-scope, claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes, documentation rewrites); factual sequencing observation, no intent attribution. Cross-references to Section 8.17 (AI-perception-management layer), EXP-183 (Part 4.2 Registry Framing Audit), Timestamp Error tab, and Kill-Shot #1 are embedded throughout. Three integration pieces required: add part8b key to sections.json; add renderSectionFromJson('part8b',...) call in build-scripts/generate-html.js; add new <li> in TOC. Word count 2326 (text-only) — over the human note's 1200-1800 target; easiest trim targets are 8b.0 preamble and 8b.6 meta if decider wants to compress. HNOTE-PART-8B-FAIR-AUDITOR-001 marked consumed in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json. Related to HNOTE-API-HUMAN-GAPS-001 (EXP-183, currently revised) — cross-references assume EXP-183's Part 4.2 ships; if EXP-184 ships first, soften the 'pending integration via EXP-183' language.
